blocks.content:
When your infrastructure grows beyond a single instance of each data source, dashboards get harder to maintain. A Prometheus server per region, a PostgreSQL database per team, or a CloudWatch account per environment means more queries that all look the same but target different backends.

The temptation is to add a query for each instance in a single panel, but this approach has limits:

- Grafana enforces a **26-query limit** per panel (A through Z)
- Every additional query increases load time
- Dashboard maintenance becomes tedious when you add or remove instances

A **data source variable** solves this by letting users select which data source instance to query from a dropdown at the top of the dashboard. You write one query, and the variable controls which backend it runs against.

This means:
- **One dashboard** serves all regions, teams, or environments
- **No query duplication** — write it once, reuse everywhere
- **Users self-serve** — they pick their instance from the dropdown without editing the dashboard

In the next milestone, you create a data source variable on a dashboard.
